verses.latin_vulgate: Tunc ergo apprehendit Pilatus Jesum, et flagellavit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τότε οὖν ἔλαβεν ὁ πιλᾶτος τὸν ἰησοῦν καὶ ἐμαστίγωσεν
verses.king_james: Then Pilate therefore took Jesus, and scourged *him*.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et milites plectentes coronam de spinis, imposuerunt capiti ejus: et veste purpurea circumdederunt eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ οἱ στρατιῶται πλέξαντες στέφανον ἐξ ἀκανθῶν ἐπέθηκαν αὐτοῦ τῇ κεφαλῇ καὶ ἱμάτιον πορφυροῦν περιέβαλον αὐτόν
verses.king_james: And the soldiers platted a crown of thorns, and put *it* on his head, and they put on him a purple robe,

verses.latin_vulgate: Et veniebant ad eum, et dicebant: Ave, rex Judæorum: et dabant ei alapas.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἔλεγον χαῖρε ὁ βασιλεὺς τῶν ἰουδαίων καὶ ἐδίδουν αὐτῷ ῥαπίσματα
verses.king_james: And said, Hail, King of the Jews! and they smote him with their hands.

verses.latin_vulgate: Exivit ergo iterum Pilatus foras, et dicit eis: Ecce adduco vobis eum foras, ut cognoscatis quia nullam invenio in eo causam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐξῆλθεν οὖν πάλιν ἔξω ὁ πιλᾶτος καὶ λέγει αὐτοῖς ἴδε ἄγω ὑμῖν αὐτὸν ἔξω ἵνα γνῶτε ὅτι ἐν αὐτῷ οὐδεμίαν αἰτίαν εὑρίσκω
verses.king_james: Pilate therefore went forth again, and saith unto them, Behold, I bring him forth to you, that ye may know that I find no fault in him.

verses.latin_vulgate: (Exivit ergo Jesus portans coronam spineam, et purpureum vestimentum.) Et dicit eis: Ecce homo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐξῆλθεν οὖν ὁ ἰησοῦς ἔξω φορῶν τὸν ἀκάνθινον στέφανον καὶ τὸ πορφυροῦν ἱμάτιον καὶ λέγει αὐτοῖς ἴδε ὁ ἄνθρωπος
verses.king_james: Then came Jesus forth, wearing the crown of thorns, and the purple robe. And *Pilate* saith unto them, Behold the man!

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum ergo vidissent eum pontifices et ministri, clamabant, dicentes: Crucifige, crucifige eum. Dicit eis Pilatus: Accipite eum vos, et crucifigite: ego enim non invenio in eo causam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅτε οὖν εἶδον αὐτὸν οἱ ἀρχιερεῖς καὶ οἱ ὑπηρέται ἐκραύγασαν λέγοντες σταύρωσον σταύρωσον λέγει αὐτοῖς ὁ πιλᾶτος λάβετε αὐτὸν ὑμεῖς καὶ σταυρώσατε ἐγὼ γὰρ οὐχ εὑρίσκω ἐν αὐτῷ αἰτίαν
verses.king_james: When the chief priests therefore and officers saw him, they cried out, saying, Crucify *him*, crucify *him*. Pilate saith unto them, Take ye him, and crucify *him*: for I find no fault in him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Responderunt ei Judæi: Nos legem habemus, et secundum legem debet mori, quia Filium Dei se fecit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπεκρίθησαν αὐτῷ οἱ ἰουδαῖοι ἡμεῖς νόμον ἔχομεν καὶ κατὰ τὸν νόμον ἡμῶν ὀφείλει ἀποθανεῖν ὅτι ἑαυτὸν υἱὸν θεοῦ ἐποίησεν
verses.king_james: The Jews answered him, We have a law, and by our law he ought to die, because he made himself the Son of God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum ergo audisset Pilatus hunc sermonem, magis timuit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅτε οὖν ἤκουσεν ὁ πιλᾶτος τοῦτον τὸν λόγον μᾶλλον ἐφοβήθη
verses.king_james: When Pilate therefore heard that saying, he was the more afraid;

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ingressus est prætorium iterum: et dixit ad Jesum: Unde es tu? Jesus autem responsum non dedit ei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εἰσῆλθεν εἰς τὸ πραιτώριον πάλιν καὶ λέγει τῷ ἰησοῦ πόθεν εἶ σύ ὁ δὲ ἰησοῦς ἀπόκρισιν οὐκ ἔδωκεν αὐτῷ
verses.king_james: And went again into the judgment hall, and saith unto Jesus, Whence art thou? But Jesus gave him no answer.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicit ergo ei Pilatus: Mihi non loqueris? nescis quia potestatem habeo crucifigere te, et potestatem habeo dimittere te?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγει οὖν αὐτῷ ὁ πιλᾶτος ἐμοὶ οὐ λαλεῖς οὐκ οἶδας ὅτι ἐξουσίαν ἔχω σταυρῶσαί σε καὶ ἐξουσίαν ἔχω ἀπολῦσαί σε
verses.king_james: Then saith Pilate unto him, Speakest thou not unto me? knowest thou not that I have power to crucify thee, and have power to release thee?

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondit Jesus: Non haberes potestatem adversum me ullam, nisi tibi datum esset desuper. Propterea qui me tradidit tibi, majus peccatum habet.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπεκρίθη ὁ ἰησοῦς οὐκ εἶχες ἐξουσίαν οὐδεμίαν κατ᾽ ἐμοῦ εἰ μὴ ἦν σοι δεδομένον ἄνωθεν διὰ τοῦτο ὁ παραδιδούς μέ σοι μείζονα ἁμαρτίαν ἔχει
verses.king_james: Jesus answered, Thou couldest have no power *at all* against me, except it were given thee from above: therefore he that delivered me unto thee hath the greater sin.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et exinde quærebat Pilatus dimittere eum. Judæi autem clamabant dicentes: Si hunc dimittis, non es amicus Cæsaris. Omnis enim qui se regem facit, contradicit Cæsari.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐκ τούτου ἐζήτει ὁ πιλᾶτος ἀπολῦσαι αὐτόν οἱ δὲ ἰουδαῖοι ἔκραζον λέγοντες ἐὰν τοῦτον ἀπολύσῃς οὐκ εἶ φίλος τοῦ καίσαρος πᾶς ὁ βασιλέα αὐτόν ποιῶν ἀντιλέγει τῷ καίσαρι
verses.king_james: And from thenceforth Pilate sought to release him: but the Jews cried out, saying, If thou let this man go, thou art not Caesar's friend: whosoever maketh himself a king speaketh against Caesar.

verses.latin_vulgate: Pilatus autem cum audisset hos sermones, adduxit foras Jesum: et sedit pro tribunali, in loco qui dicitur Lithostrotos, hebraice autem Gabbatha.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ οὖν πιλᾶτος ἀκούσας τοῦτον τὸν λογὸν, ἤγαγεν ἔξω τὸν ἰησοῦν καὶ ἐκάθισεν ἐπὶ τοῦ βήματος εἰς τόπον λεγόμενον λιθόστρωτον ἑβραϊστὶ δὲ γαββαθα
verses.king_james: When Pilate therefore heard that saying, he brought Jesus forth, and sat down in the judgment seat in a place that is called the Pavement, but in the Hebrew, Gabbatha.

verses.latin_vulgate: Erat enim parasceve Paschæ, hora quasi sexta, et dicit Judæis: Ecce rex vester.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἦν δὲ παρασκευὴ τοῦ πάσχα ὥρα δὲ ὡσεὶ ἕκτη καὶ λέγει τοῖς ἰουδαίοις ἴδε ὁ βασιλεὺς ὑμῶν
verses.king_james: And it was the preparation of the passover, and about the sixth hour: and he saith unto the Jews, Behold your King!

verses.latin_vulgate: Illi autem clamabant: Tolle, tolle, crucifige eum. Dicit eis Pilatus: Regem vestrum crucifigam? Responderunt pontifices: Non habemus regem, nisi Cæsarem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἱ δὲ ἐκραύγασαν ἆρον ἆρον σταύρωσον αὐτόν λέγει αὐτοῖς ὁ πιλᾶτος τὸν βασιλέα ὑμῶν σταυρώσω ἀπεκρίθησαν οἱ ἀρχιερεῖς οὐκ ἔχομεν βασιλέα εἰ μὴ καίσαρα
verses.king_james: But they cried out, Away with *him*, away with *him*, crucify him. Pilate saith unto them, Shall I crucify your King? The chief priests answered, We have no king but Caesar.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tunc ergo tradidit eis illum ut crucifigeretur. Susceperunt autem Jesum, et eduxerunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τότε οὖν παρέδωκεν αὐτὸν αὐτοῖς ἵνα σταυρωθῇ παρέλαβον δὲ τὸν ἰησοῦν καὶ ἀπήγαγον
verses.king_james: Then delivered he him therefore unto them to be crucified. And they took Jesus, and led *him* away.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et bajulans sibi crucem exivit in eum, qui dicitur Calvariæ locum, hebraice autem Golgotha:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ βαστάζων τὸν σταυρὸν αὐτοῦ ἐξῆλθεν εἰς τὸν λεγόμενον κρανίου τόπον ὃς λέγεται ἑβραϊστὶ γολγοθα
verses.king_james: And he bearing his cross went forth into a place called *the place* of a skull, which is called in the Hebrew Golgotha:

verses.latin_vulgate: ubi crucifixerunt eum, et cum eo alios duos hinc et hinc, medium autem Jesum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅπου αὐτὸν ἐσταύρωσαν καὶ μετ᾽ αὐτοῦ ἄλλους δύο ἐντεῦθεν καὶ ἐντεῦθεν μέσον δὲ τὸν ἰησοῦν
verses.king_james: Where they crucified him, and two other with him, on either side one, and Jesus in the midst.

verses.latin_vulgate: Scripsit autem et titulum Pilatus, et posuit super crucem. Erat autem scriptum: Jesus Nazarenus, Rex Judæorum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔγραψεν δὲ καὶ τίτλον ὁ πιλᾶτος καὶ ἔθηκεν ἐπὶ τοῦ σταυροῦ ἦν δὲ γεγραμμένον ἰησοῦς ὁ ναζωραῖος ὁ βασιλεὺς τῶν ἰουδαίων
verses.king_james: And Pilate wrote a title, and put *it* on the cross. And the writing was, JESUS OF NAZARETH THE KING OF THE JEWS.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hunc ergo titulum multi Judæorum legerunt: quia prope civitatem erat locus, ubi crucifixus est Jesus, et erat scriptum hebraice, græce, et latine.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τοῦτον οὖν τὸν τίτλον πολλοὶ ἀνέγνωσαν τῶν ἰουδαίων ὅτι ἐγγὺς ἦν τῆς πόλεως ὁ τόπος ὅπου ἐσταυρώθη ὁ ἰησοῦς καὶ ἦν γεγραμμένον ἑβραϊστί ἑλληνιστί ῥωμαϊστί
verses.king_james: This title then read many of the Jews: for the place where Jesus was crucified was nigh to the city: and it was written in Hebrew, *and* Greek, *and* Latin.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicebant ergo Pilato pontifices Judæorum: Noli scribere: Rex Judæorum: sed quia ipse dixit: Rex sum Judæorum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔλεγον οὖν τῷ πιλάτῳ οἱ ἀρχιερεῖς τῶν ἰουδαίων μὴ γράφε ὁ βασιλεὺς τῶν ἰουδαίων ἀλλ᾽ ὅτι ἐκεῖνος εἶπεν βασιλεύς εἰμι τῶν ἰουδαίων
verses.king_james: Then said the chief priests of the Jews to Pilate, Write not, The King of the Jews; but that he said, I am King of the Jews.

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondit Pilatus: Quod scripsi, scripsi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπεκρίθη ὁ πιλᾶτος ὅ γέγραφα γέγραφα
verses.king_james: Pilate answered, What I have written I have written.

verses.latin_vulgate: Milites ergo cum crucifixissent eum, acceperunt vestimenta ejus (et fecerunt quatuor partes, unicuique militi partem) et tunicam. Erat autem tunica inconsutilis, desuper contexta per totum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἱ οὖν στρατιῶται ὅτε ἐσταύρωσαν τὸν ἰησοῦν ἔλαβον τὰ ἱμάτια αὐτοῦ καὶ ἐποίησαν τέσσαρα μέρη ἑκάστῳ στρατιώτῃ μέρος καὶ τὸν χιτῶνα ἦν δὲ ὁ χιτὼν ἄῤῥαφος, ἐκ τῶν ἄνωθεν ὑφαντὸς δι᾽ ὅλου
verses.king_james: Then the soldiers, when they had crucified Jesus, took his garments, and made four parts, to every soldier a part; and also *his* coat: now the coat was without seam, woven from the top throughout.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixerunt ergo ad invicem: Non scindamus eam, sed sortiamur de illa cujus sit. Ut Scriptura impleretur, dicens: Partiti sunt vestimenta mea sibi: et in vestem meam miserunt sortem. Et milites quidem hæc fecerunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπον οὖν πρὸς ἀλλήλους μὴ σχίσωμεν αὐτόν ἀλλὰ λάχωμεν περὶ αὐτοῦ τίνος ἔσται ἵνα ἡ γραφὴ πληρωθῇ ἡ λέγουσα διεμερίσαντο τὰ ἱμάτιά μου ἑαυτοῖς καὶ ἐπὶ τὸν ἱματισμόν μου ἔβαλον κλῆρον οἱ μὲν οὖν στρατιῶται ταῦτα ἐποίησαν
verses.king_james: They said therefore among themselves, Let us not rend it, but cast lots for it, whose it shall be: that the scripture might be fulfilled, which saith, They parted my raiment among them, and for my vesture they did cast lots. These things therefore the soldiers did.

verses.latin_vulgate: Stabant autem juxta crucem Jesu mater ejus, et soror matris ejus, Maria Cleophæ, et Maria Magdalene.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἱστήκεισαν δὲ παρὰ τῷ σταυρῷ τοῦ ἰησοῦ ἡ μήτηρ αὐτοῦ καὶ ἡ ἀδελφὴ τῆς μητρὸς αὐτοῦ μαρία ἡ τοῦ κλωπᾶ καὶ μαρία ἡ μαγδαληνή
verses.king_james: Now there stood by the cross of Jesus his mother, and his mother's sister, Mary the *wife* of Cleophas, and Mary Magdalene.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum vidisset ergo Jesus matrem, et discipulum stantem, quem diligebat, dicit matri suæ: Mulier, ecce filius tuus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἰησοῦς οὖν ἰδὼν τὴν μητέρα καὶ τὸν μαθητὴν παρεστῶτα ὃν ἠγάπα λέγει τῇ μητρί αὐτοῦ, γύναι ἰδοῦ ὁ υἱός σου
verses.king_james: When Jesus therefore saw his mother, and the disciple standing by, whom he loved, he saith unto his mother, Woman, behold thy son!

verses.latin_vulgate: Deinde dicit discipulo: Ecce mater tua. Et ex illa hora accepit eam discipulus in sua.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶτα λέγει τῷ μαθητῇ ἰδού, ἡ μήτηρ σου καὶ ἀπ᾽ ἐκείνης τῆς ὥρας ἔλαβεν αὐτὴν ὁ μαθητὴς εἰς τὰ ἴδια
verses.king_james: Then saith he to the disciple, Behold thy mother! And from that hour that disciple took her unto his own *home*.

verses.latin_vulgate: Postea sciens Jesus quia omnia consummata sunt, ut consummaretur Scriptura, dixit: Sitio.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μετὰ τοῦτο εἰδὼς ὁ ἰησοῦς ὅτι πάντα ἤδη τετέλεσται ἵνα τελειωθῇ ἡ γραφή λέγει διψῶ
verses.king_james: After this, Jesus knowing that all things were now accomplished, that the scripture might be fulfilled, saith, I thirst.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vas ergo erat positum aceto plenum. Illi autem spongiam plenam aceto, hyssopo circumponentes, obtulerunt ori ejus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: σκεῦος οὖν ἔκειτο ὄξους μεστόν οἱ δὲ, πλήσαντες σπόγγον ὄξους καὶ ὑσσώπῳ περιθέντες προσήνεγκαν αὐτοῦ τῷ στόματι
verses.king_james: Now there was set a vessel full of vinegar: and they filled a spunge with vinegar, and put *it* upon hyssop, and put *it* to his mouth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum ergo accepisset Jesus acetum, dixit: Consummatum est. Et inclinato capite tradidit spiritum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅτε οὖν ἔλαβεν τὸ ὄξος ὁ ἰησοῦς εἶπεν τετέλεσται καὶ κλίνας τὴν κεφαλὴν παρέδωκεν τὸ πνεῦμα
verses.king_james: When Jesus therefore had received the vinegar, he said, It is finished: and he bowed his head, and gave up the ghost.

verses.latin_vulgate: Judæi ergo (quoniam parasceve erat) ut non remanerent in cruce corpora sabbato (erat enim magnus dies ille sabbati), rogaverunt Pilatum ut frangerentur eorum crura, et tollerentur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἱ οὖν ἰουδαῖοι ἵνα μὴ μείνῃ ἐπὶ τοῦ σταυροῦ τὰ σώματα ἐν τῷ σαββάτῳ ἐπεὶ παρασκευὴ ἦν ἦν γὰρ μεγάλη ἡ ἡμέρα ἐκείνου τοῦ σαββάτου ἠρώτησαν τὸν πιλᾶτον ἵνα κατεαγῶσιν αὐτῶν τὰ σκέλη καὶ ἀρθῶσιν
verses.king_james: The Jews therefore, because it was the preparation, that the bodies should not remain upon the cross on the sabbath day, (for that sabbath day was an high day,) besought Pilate that their legs might be broken, and *that* they might be taken away.

verses.latin_vulgate: Venerunt ergo milites: et primi quidem fregerunt crura, et alterius, qui crucifixus est cum eo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἦλθον οὖν οἱ στρατιῶται καὶ τοῦ μὲν πρώτου κατέαξαν τὰ σκέλη καὶ τοῦ ἄλλου τοῦ συσταυρωθέντος αὐτῷ
verses.king_james: Then came the soldiers, and brake the legs of the first, and of the other which was crucified with him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ad Jesum autem cum venissent, ut viderunt eum jam mortuum, non fregerunt ejus crura,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐπὶ δὲ τὸν ἰησοῦν ἐλθόντες ὡς εἶδον αὐτὸν ἤδη τεθνηκότα οὐ κατέαξαν αὐτοῦ τὰ σκέλη
verses.king_james: But when they came to Jesus, and saw that he was dead already, they brake not his legs:

verses.latin_vulgate: sed unus militum lancea latus ejus aperuit, et continuo exivit sanguis et aqua.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀλλ᾽ εἷς τῶν στρατιωτῶν λόγχῃ αὐτοῦ τὴν πλευρὰν ἔνυξεν καὶ εὐθὺς ἐξῆλθεν αἷμα καὶ ὕδωρ
verses.king_james: But one of the soldiers with a spear pierced his side, and forthwith came there out blood and water.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et qui vidit, testimonium perhibuit: et verum est testimonium ejus. Et ille scit quia vera dicit: ut et vos credatis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ὁ ἑωρακὼς μεμαρτύρηκεν καὶ ἀληθινὴ αὐτοῦ ἐστιν ἡ μαρτυρία κἀκεῖνος οἶδεν ὅτι ἀληθῆ λέγει ἵνα ὑμεῖς πιστεύσητε
verses.king_james: And he that saw *it* bare record, and his record is true: and he knoweth that he saith true, that ye might believe.

verses.latin_vulgate: Facta sunt enim hæc ut Scriptura impleretur: Os non comminuetis ex eo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐγένετο γὰρ ταῦτα ἵνα ἡ γραφὴ πληρωθῇ ὀστοῦν οὐ συντριβήσεται αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: For these things were done, that the scripture should be fulfilled, A bone of him shall not be broken.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et iterum alia Scriptura dicit: Videbunt in quem transfixerunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ πάλιν ἑτέρα γραφὴ λέγει ὄψονται εἰς ὃν ἐξεκέντησαν
verses.king_james: And again another scripture saith, They shall look on him whom they pierced.

verses.latin_vulgate: Post hæc autem rogavit Pilatum Joseph ab Arimathæa (eo quod esset discipulus Jesu, occultus autem propter metum Judæorum), ut tolleret corpus Jesu. Et permisit Pilatus. Venit ergo, et tulit corpus Jesu.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μετὰ δὲ ταῦτα ἠρώτησεν τὸν πιλᾶτον ὁ ἰωσὴφ ὁ ἀπὸ Ἁριμαθαίας ὢν μαθητὴς τοῦ ἰησοῦ κεκρυμμένος δὲ διὰ τὸν φόβον τῶν ἰουδαίων ἵνα ἄρῃ τὸ σῶμα τοῦ ἰησοῦ καὶ ἐπέτρεψεν ὁ πιλᾶτος ἦλθεν οὖν καὶ ἦρεν τὸ σῶμα τοῦ ἰησοῦ
verses.king_james: And after this Joseph of Arimathaea, being a disciple of Jesus, but secretly for fear of the Jews, besought Pilate that he might take away the body of Jesus: and Pilate gave *him* leave. He came therefore, and took the body of Jesus.

verses.latin_vulgate: Venit autem et Nicodemus, qui venerat ad Jesum nocte primum, ferens mixturam myrrhæ et aloës, quasi libras centum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἦλθεν δὲ καὶ νικόδημος ὁ ἐλθὼν πρὸς τὸν ἰησοῦν νυκτὸς τὸ πρῶτον φέρων μίγμα σμύρνης καὶ ἀλόης ὡσεὶ λίτρας ἑκατόν
verses.king_james: And there came also Nicodemus, which at the first came to Jesus by night, and brought a mixture of myrrh and aloes, about an hundred pound *weight*.

verses.latin_vulgate: Acceperunt ergo corpus Jesu, et ligaverunt illud linteis cum aromatibus, sicut mos est Judæis sepelire.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔλαβον οὖν τὸ σῶμα τοῦ ἰησοῦ καὶ ἔδησαν αὐτὸ ὀθονίοις μετὰ τῶν ἀρωμάτων καθὼς ἔθος ἐστὶν τοῖς ἰουδαίοις ἐνταφιάζειν
verses.king_james: Then took they the body of Jesus, and wound it in linen clothes with the spices, as the manner of the Jews is to bury.

verses.latin_vulgate: Erat autem in loco, ubi crucifixus est, hortus: et in horto monumentum novum, in quo nondum quisquam positus erat.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἦν δὲ ἐν τῷ τόπῳ ὅπου ἐσταυρώθη κῆπος καὶ ἐν τῷ κήπῳ μνημεῖον καινὸν ἐν ᾧ οὐδέπω οὐδεὶς ἐτέθη
verses.king_james: Now in the place where he was crucified there was a garden; and in the garden a new sepulchre, wherein was never man yet laid.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ibi ergo propter parasceven Judæorum, quia juxta erat monumentum, posuerunt Jesum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐκεῖ οὖν διὰ τὴν παρασκευὴν τῶν ἰουδαίων ὅτι ἐγγὺς ἦν τὸ μνημεῖον ἔθηκαν τὸν ἰησοῦν
verses.king_james: There laid they Jesus therefore because of the Jews' preparation *day*; for the sepulchre was nigh at hand.
